paper_title: ELT pedagogical reforms: EFL high-school teachers' perceptions and responses

entities.description: Educational changes focused on transforming English language teaching pedagogy from traditional to new forms.
The overall understanding, beliefs, and awareness of high-school teachers regarding pedagogical reforms.
A research design combining both quantitative and qualitative methods (questionnaire and interviews).
A tool consisting of 34 items using Likert-scales sent via Google Form to collect data.
Interviews conducted online via Zalo, Zoom, and Skype to gain deeper insights.
Statistical test used to compare the mean scores against a test value of 3.50.
Quantitative and qualitative data collected from 102 teachers in the Mekong Delta.
Relatively high score indicating teachers' awareness of reforms.
High level of knowledge regarding definitions and needs for ELT reforms.
Metric indicating that teachers' actual implementation is above average but lower than understanding.
Statistical significance level used to validate the results.
The field of education focused on teaching English to non-native speakers.
Master student and lead researcher of the study.
The primary university affiliation for the authors in Vietnam.

entities.id: School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University
entities.description: The specific academic unit of the corresponding author.

relations.target: School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University
